name: Acidic Rain
tips: A hard counter to Robot/Metal decks. Every Diodebot, RGB Lasercannon, Surgefed Sparkbot, and Dark Matter Battery on the board becomes burning simultaneously — each then takes burn tick damage in its owner's Decay Phase.
Burn is a boolean, not a stack — re-applying to an already-burning minion does nothing. Cast when several Metal/Robot targets are fresh.

Paired with Flame Wyrm, each burning ENEMY Robot/Machine/Metal minion ticks for 6🤍 in its owner's Decay Phase instead of 5🤍. The aura never boosts burns on your own minions — your own burned Robots still tick for 5🤍.
Burns your OWN Robots too — don't cast it if you're the Metal deck. Water vs Metal is the intended matchup.
The 2-mana react mode is cheap cycling. Keep Acidic Rain in hand against control opponents — every turn they end lets you convert 2 mana into a card during their Decay Phase.
rulings: Magic mode (5 mana): burns every living minion on the board — both sides — whose tribe is Robot or Machine, OR whose element is Metal. A Blue Diodebot (Robot + Metal) is still burned only once (burn is boolean).
The tribe/element filter is an OR: a Fire-element Robot still qualifies, as does a Metal-element non-Robot.
Does not damage players. Burn only affects minions.

React mode (2 mana): playable only in the react window at the end of the opponent's turn — their Decay Phase, after all automatic end-of-turn effects resolve. Draws exactly 1 card for the reacting player.
React mode does NOT burn anything — only the draw effect fires. Magic mode does NOT draw — only the burns fire. The two modes are mutually exclusive per cast.
